tex:
Nadira owns a clothes shop.

(a) The pictogram shows the number of skirts that were sold each day in one week.

\begin{InsertScreenShot}

\end{InsertScreenShot}

(i) (b) On which day were most skirts sold?

(c) How many skirts were sold on Wednesday?

(d) Work out how many more skirts were sold on Friday than on Thursday.

(e) The shop is open for 6 days each week.\\
On each day, the shop is open from 09 30 until 13 00 and from 14 15 until 20 30.\\
Work out the total number of hours the shop is open in one week.

(f) Nadira pays 6 people to work in the shop.\\
In one week
\begin{parts}

(g) 4 people each work for 38 hours

(h) 2 people each work for 25 hours.
They are each paid \$11.40 for each hour they work.\\
Calculate the total amount Nadira pays these 6 people in one week.

Nadira has some T-shirts that are either white or blue or green.\\
The numbers of T-shirts are in the ratio white : blue : green = 5 : 4 : 1.\\
48 of the T-shirts are blue.\\
Work out the total number of T-shirts.

Nadira buys a pack of 40 dresses and pays \$500.\\
She sells 35 of these dresses for \$22 each.\\
She sells the remaining 5 dresses for \$14.50 each.\\
Calculate the percentage profit she makes when she sells all 40 dresses.

\end{parts}

Henry decorates a room.

(a) Complete Henry's shopping bill.

\begin{table}[h]
\centering
\begin{tabular}{|l|c|}
\hline
\multicolumn{1}{|c|}{Item} & Cost (\$) \\ \hline
3 tins of paint at \$15.95 each &  \\ \hline
2 brushes at \$7.50 each &  \\ \hline
1 roll of tape at \$2.90 & 2.90 \\ \hline
\multicolumn{1}{|r|}{Total} &  \\ \hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

(b) \begin{InsertScreenShot}

\end{InsertScreenShot}

The diagram shows the floor of the room.
(i) (c) Calculate the area of the floor.

(d) Henry buys varnish for the floor of the room.\\
500 ml of varnish covers 8 m$^2$ of floor.\\
Calculate the amount of varnish Henry needs.

(e) This scale drawing shows the window in the room.\\
The scale is 1 centimetre represents 40 centimetres.

\begin{InsertScreenShot}

\end{InsertScreenShot}

Work out the actual length and height of the window.

(f) \begin{InsertScreenShot}

\end{InsertScreenShot}

The diagram shows one wall of the room.
Calculate the area of the wall.

(g) Henry buys a circular mirror for the room.\\
The diameter of the mirror is 80 cm.\\
Calculate the circumference of the mirror.
texHtml:
Henry decorates a room. (a) Complete Henry's shopping bill. \begin{table}[h]
ItemCost (\$)
3 tins of paint at \$15.95 each
2 brushes at \$7.50 each
1 roll of tape at \$2.902.90
Total
\end{table} (b) The diagram shows the floor of the room. (i) (c) Calculate the area of the floor. (d) Henry buys varnish for the floor of the room.\\ 500 ml of varnish covers 8 m$^2$ of floor.\\ Calculate the amount of varnish Henry needs. (e) This scale drawing shows the window in the room.\\ The scale is 1 centimetre represents 40 centimetres. Work out the actual length and height of the window. (f) The diagram shows one wall of the room. Calculate the area of the wall. (g) Henry buys a circular mirror for the room.\\ The diameter of the mirror is 80 cm.\\ Calculate the circumference of the mirror.

tex:
(a) Write down
(i) (b) all the factors of 18,

(c) a square number between 30 and 50,

(d) a prime number between 90 and 100.

(e) Put one pair of brackets into each calculation to make it correct.
(i) (f) $24 \div 6 + 2 \times 3 = 9$

(g) $24 \div 6 + 2 \times 3 = 2$

(h) Calculate.
\[ \dfrac{4.85 \times 6.14}{8.91 + 3.89} \]
Give your answer correct to 2 decimal places.

(i) (i) (j) Find the highest common factor (HCF) of 36 and 90.

(k) Find the lowest common multiple (LCM) of 36 and 90.

(l) (i) (m) Write $4.2 \times 10^{-3}$ as an ordinary number.

(n) Calculate $(8.1 \times 10^5) + (7.9 \times 10^4)$.\\
Give your answer in standard form.

(a) 50 students each record the number of glasses of water they drink in one day.\\
The results for 10 of the students are shown below.
2 \quad 1 \quad 5 \quad 1 \quad 3 \quad 2 \quad 1 \quad 0 \quad 0 \quad 1
The results for the remaining 40 students are recorded in the table.\\
Complete the table to show the results for all 50 students.

(i) (b) \begin{table}[h]
\centering
\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|}
\hline
Number of glasses of water & Tally & Frequency \\ \hline
0 & $|||| \ |$ &  \\ \hline
1 & $||$ &  \\ \hline
2 & $|||| \ |||$ &  \\ \hline
3 & $|||| \ |||| \ |$ &  \\ \hline
4 & $|||| \ |||$ &  \\ \hline
5 & $||||$ &  \\ \hline
\multicolumn{2}{|r|}{Total} & 50 \\ \hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

(c) Write down the range.

(d) Find the median.

(e) Find the percentage of the 50 students who drink 4 glasses of water.

(f) One of the 50 students is chosen at random.\\
Find the probability that this student drinks fewer than 2 glasses of water in one day.\\
Give your answer as a fraction in its lowest terms.

(g) Musa has a glass that holds 250 ml of water.\\
He drinks 5 of these glasses of water.\\
He fills his glass from a 2-litre bottle of water.\\
Work out how much water is left in the bottle.\\
Give your answer in millilitres.

(h) The amount of water, $w$ litres, in a jug is 1.5 litres, correct to the nearest 0.1 litre.\\
Complete this statement about the value of $w$.

(i) \begin{InsertScreenShot}

\end{InsertScreenShot}

Another glass is in the shape of a cylinder.\\
The cylinder has height 15 cm and diameter 7 cm.\\
Calculate the volume of the glass.

(a) 50 students each record the number of glasses of water they drink in one day.\\ The results for 10 of the students are shown below. 2 \quad 1 \quad 5 \quad 1 \quad 3 \quad 2 \quad 1 \quad 0 \quad 0 \quad 1 The results for the remaining 40 students are recorded in the table.\\ Complete the table to show the results for all 50 students. (i) (b) \begin{table}[h]

0$|||| \ |$

5$||||$
Total50
\end{table} (c) Write down the range. (d) Find the median. (e) Find the percentage of the 50 students who drink 4 glasses of water. (f) One of the 50 students is chosen at random.\\ Find the probability that this student drinks fewer than 2 glasses of water in one day.\\ Give your answer as a fraction in its lowest terms. (g) Musa has a glass that holds 250 ml of water.\\ He drinks 5 of these glasses of water.\\ He fills his glass from a 2-litre bottle of water.\\ Work out how much water is left in the bottle.\\ Give your answer in millilitres. (h) The amount of water, $w$ litres, in a jug is 1.5 litres, correct to the nearest 0.1 litre.\\ Complete this statement about the value of $w$. (i) Another glass is in the shape of a cylinder.\\ The cylinder has height 15 cm and diameter 7 cm.\\ Calculate the volume of the glass.

(a) Complete the table of values for $y = x^2 - 5x + 3$.
$x$$-1$0123456
$y$$-1$$-3$$-3$$-1$3
(b) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = x^2 - 5x + 3$ for $-1 \leqslant x \leqslant 6$. (c) Use your graph to solve the equation $x^2 - 5x + 3 = 0$.

tex:
(a) Here are the first four terms of a sequence.
32 \quad 27 \quad 22 \quad 17
(i) (b) Write down the next term.

(c) Write down the rule for continuing the sequence.

(d) The $n$th term of another sequence is $n^2 + 2n$. \\
Find the first three terms of this sequence.

(e) Here are the first three patterns in a sequence.
\begin{InsertScreenShot}

\end{InsertScreenShot}

(i) (f) Complete the table.
\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Pattern & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 & 5 \\ \hline
Number of lines & 6 & & & & \\ \hline
\end{tabular}

(g) Find an expression, in terms of $n$, for the number of lines in Pattern $n$.

(h) Jake says that he can make one of these patterns using exactly 105 lines. \\
Explain, without doing any working, why he is wrong.
texHtml:

(d) The $n$th term of another sequence is $n^2 + 2n$. \\ Find the first three terms of this sequence. (e) Here are the first three patterns in a sequence. (i) (f) Complete the table.
Pattern12345
Number of lines6
(g) Find an expression, in terms of $n$, for the number of lines in Pattern $n$. (h) Jake says that he can make one of these patterns using exactly 105 lines. \\ Explain, without doing any working, why he is wrong.

tex:
The diagram shows two triangles, $A$ and $B$, and two points $P$ and $Q$.

\begin{InsertScreenShot}

\end{InsertScreenShot}

(a) (i) (b) Write down the co-ordinates of point $P$.

(c) Write down the column vector $\overrightarrow{PQ}$.

(d) (i) (e) Describe fully the single transformation that maps triangle $A$ onto triangle $B$.

(f) On the grid, draw the image of triangle $A$ after a translation by the vector $\begin{pmatrix} 4 \\ -2 \end{pmatrix}$.

(g) On the grid, draw the image of triangle $A$ after a rotation through $90^\circ$ clockwise about $(0, 0)$.

(a) $c = 5a - 2b$
(i) (b) Find the value of $c$ when $a = 8$ and $b = -3$.

(c) Make $a$ the subject of the formula $c = 5a - 2b$.

(d) Factorise $3x + 12$.

(e) Expand $x(2y + x)$.

(f) Cara has $n$ pencils.\\
Alice has twice as many pencils as Cara.\\
Leon has three more pencils than Alice.\\
The three children have a total of 58 pencils.\\
Use this information to write down an equation and solve it to find the value of $n$.
